description: 'wethate' is garments and household cloth gathered for washing or moving through drying and folding afterwards. It may refer to one load or to the material waiting at a particular stage. 'wethalu' (garment) names something worn; laundry gathers cloth by the work currently being done to it.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then move into true-mid front 'e'. Place the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th' and open into stressed central 'a'. Return the tongue tip to the backs of the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't' and release into final true-mid front 'e'.
sound_symbolism: 'wetha' (cloth) reaches the stressed centre intact. Breath at the teeth opens through 'tha', then final 'te' returns to the same place with a clean stop. The cloth family passes through an open middle and comes back ready for its next stage.
usage_notes: Laundry is usually mass-like, while separate loads may be counted. The concrete washing, drying, or folding action belongs in its own clause. Work terms can describe assignment and burden without changing what the gathered cloth is.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Calling the gathered cloth laundry gives repeated domestic work an object that people can share, assign, accept, or refuse.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Washing day followed water, weather, fuel, and the time cloth needed to dry before night.

semantic_domains.activity: cloth moving through washing, drying, or folding
semantic_domains.community: a recurring body of domestic work within a household
semantic_domains.physical: garments and household cloth gathered as one load
